name: 1983 Kuwait Bombings
description: On 12 December 1983, a series of six coordinated truck bombings struck the US and French embassies, a petroleum refinery, the airport, and other targets in Kuwait City, killing six people and injuring more than eighty. The attacks were carried out by Iranian-backed operatives affiliated with the Iraqi Dawa Party. [Abu Mahdi al-Muhandis](/people/abu-mahdi-al-muhandis), later the deputy commander of Iraq's Popular Mobilisation Forces, was convicted in absentia and sentenced to death by a Kuwaiti court for his role in planning the bombings. Seventeen perpetrators were arrested and became known as the 'Kuwait 17'; [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) later attempted multiple operations to secure their release.

sources.title: The Iraqi Commander Who Was Killed In U.S. Drone Strike: Abu Mahdi Al-Muhandis

editorial_notes: Six killed, 80+ injured. Al-Muhandis sentenced to death in absentia by Kuwait in 2007 (retroactive conviction). Kuwait 17 prisoners were later freed during 1990 Iraqi invasion.
